summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #514 where local LLM model validation timeouts (10s) were blocking model saves, even when the model eventually responds successfully after loading into memory.
summary.outcome: Increased backend probe timeouts to 30s, made frontend treat timeouts as non-blocking (saving model with warning instead of blocking), added 'Slow' badge distinction from 'Unsupported', wrote regression tests that pass, committed and pushed with PR creation.

repo: Two separate timeout constants exist: `models.test` RPC timeout in crates/chat/src/lib.rs:2231 and VALIDATION_PROBE_TIMEOUT_SECS in crates/provider-setup/src/lib.rs:2496, both were 10s
Single-model save flow in providers.js:824 blocked saves on test timeout; multi-model selector in providers.js:1123 showed 'Unsupported' on timeout — two distinct UX problems from same root cause
Beads issue tracker was unavailable (DB down) during the session
Project uses biome for JS linting/formatting and cargo fmt + clippy for Rust
E2E tests live in crates/web/ui/e2e/specs/ and use Playwright-style specs; mocking RPC for timeout scenarios is complex so unit tests were preferred
ModelService trait is defined in crates/service-traits/src/lib.rs

code.finding: models.test RPC timeout constant — was 10s, increased to 30s
Single-model save flow: testModel() timeout blocked save; fixed to treat timeout like modelServiceUnavailable
Multi-model selector probe: timeout showed red 'Unsupported' badge; fixed to show neutral 'Slow' badge with tooltip
Added isTimeoutError() helper to distinguish timeout errors from other failures; exported for use in providers.js

workflow: For slow async regression tests (30s timeout test), use a longer tokio::test timeout annotation to avoid false failures
Running cargo fmt before clippy avoids noise; formatting issues can mask clippy output
SlowStartProvider pattern (provider that sleeps N seconds before first token) is useful for testing model-loading latency scenarios

friction: Beads issue tracker DB was down, preventing issue creation for tracking
Multiple iterations needed to get the Rust regression test to compile — CompletionResponse struct shape required exploration across multiple crates before finding it in crates/agents/src/model.rs
The 30s timeout regression test takes real wall-clock time to run, slowing the feedback loop
ModelService trait location required 6+ grep attempts before finding it in crates/service-traits/src/lib.rs
open_items: E2E test for the frontend timeout-non-blocking behavior was deferred — mocking RPC timeout in Playwright tests is complex and was deemed lower priority than unit tests
The 'Slow' badge tooltip says 'may still work' which is vague — could be improved with more specific guidance on what slow means for local LLMs
No provider-setup integration test was added for the VALIDATION_PROBE_TIMEOUT_SECS change — only the chat/lib.rs path has regression coverage
Consider exposing timeout values as user-configurable settings for power users running very large local models that need >30s load time
